result: A five-record chronology now links queue intent, implementation commits, evidence commits, acceptance boundaries, unresolved external outcomes, and proposed next actions without treating commit order as causal proof.
contradictions_resolved: Scoped item completion can coexist with later domain work.
Evidence commits prove durable records exist but do not prove verifier execution, deployment, or runtime outcome.
The older aggregate queue and later additive owner updates represent stale snapshot plus subsequent observations, not mutually exclusive history.
privacy_boundary: Public repository files, public commit metadata, and abstract project relations only; raw private chat, health material, credentials, identifying data, and unreviewed uploads excluded.

verification.method: GitHub create-file acknowledgement plus source SHA and recent commit-order cross-check
verification.limits: No runtime verifier was executed; this evidence records committed structure, not external outcomes.
